stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Zelenskyy seeks 300 Patriots to get through winter
stories.summary: Kyiv to anchor European alternative missile defence system called Freyja; more sanctions against Russian state hacking outfits. What we know on day 1,602 Volodymyr Zelenskyy has welcomed an agreement with allies ⁠for ​a European-backed anti-ballistic missile shield as an alternative ⁠to the US Patriot system – but the Ukrainian president stressed that Kyiv ⁠still needs the American interceptors for ​now and ‌the coming ‌winter . Zelenskyy said the Freyja ‌defence project, originated in Ukraine,

stories.title: Trump notifies Congress of new war against Iran
stories.summary: President Donald Trump formally notified lawmakers this weekend that the nation is once again at war with Iran, giving his administration another 60-day clock to use the military in the region without congressional approval. In a letter to Congress dated July 10, obtained by POLITICO, Trump stated that the strikes that began on July 7 represent “military action consistent with my responsibility to protect Americans and United States’ interests both at home and abroad.” The on

stories.title: Crimea was Russia’s stronghold. Now it’s a vulnerability.
stories.summary: Oleksandra warned her parents in Crimea to stock up on emergency supplies weeks ago. But her mother didn’t take the warning seriously. Crimea was supposed to be Russian President Vladimir Putin’s crowning achievement — a stronghold in the war against Ukraine, a launchpad to project Russia’s military power across the Black Sea and a domestic symbol of his imperial ambitions. All of that is now under threat, with Ukraine gaining air control over routes into the Russian-occupied peninsula, targetin

stories.title: Michigan health officials identify ‘potential source’ of parasite outbreak
stories.summary: Authorities said inquiry into cyclosporiasis outbreak was continuing but early results pointed to salad greens and lettuce What is cyclosporiasis, the parasitic illness causing ‘explosive’ diarrhea? State health officials in Michigan said on Monday they have identified lettuces and salad greens as a “potential source” of the thousands of cases of cyclosporiasis – a parasitic infection that causes “watery diarrhea”, loss of appetite and weight loss. In a statement, the Michigan department of heal

stories.title: Hungary parliament votes to remove president from office
stories.summary: Tamás Sulyok was widely seen as a loyalist of former prime minister Viktor Orbán, who lost power in April after 16 years.

stories.title: Hungary: Lawmakers pass law to oust Orban ally president
stories.summary: The Hungarian parliament continued its attempts to leave the Viktor Orban era behind the country by moving to oust President Tamas Sulyok.

stories.title: A tűz olyan gyorsan terjedt, hogy már a telkem szélén volt, és elzárta az egyetlen kiutat
stories.summary: A spanyoloknál 13 halálos áldozatot követeltek az erdőtüzek. A túlélők közül volt, aki a saját házában küzdött meg az épületet bekerítő lángokkal. A Párizs melletti erdőben olyan gyorsan terjednek a lángok, hogy hetekbe is eltelhet a megfékezésük. Európa a hőség mellett a pusztító erdőtüzekkel is elkeseredetten küzd.

stories.title: Rákbetegségéről írt a közösségi oldalán Kovács Ágnes olimpiai bajnok úszó
stories.summary: Kovács hétfőn lett 45 éves, posztjában azt írta, azért osztja meg a történetét, mert így akarja felhívni a figyelmet a szűrővizsgálatok fontosságára.

stories.title: A vendégmunkásvita a magyar ipar egyik érzékeny pontját találta el
stories.summary: Nehezen tudnak alkalmazkodni a cégek ahhoz, ha hirtelen kihúzzák alóluk a munkaerő-utánpótlás külföldi bázisát. A gépesítéssel mindenki számol, de még nem lehet az összes munkafázist kiváltani.

stories.title: Maradona keze mellett egy utolsó pillanatos mezcsere is kellett az angolok legyőzéséhez 1986-ban
stories.summary: Az argentin futballcsapat egy mexikóvárosi boltból szerezte be a mezt, amiben pályára lépett az 1986-os vb negyeddöntőjében. A legenda szerint a meccsen két emblematikus gólt szerző Maradona bökött rá arra a darabra, amit sufnimódszerekkel hoztak meccsképes állapotba.

stories.title: Napos, néhol gomolyfelhős nyári meleg várható, 30 fok fölötti hőmérséklettel
stories.summary: Az esőre még mindig várni kell.

stories.title: „Annyira nem lehet bolond senki, hogy ilyen gyorsan ennyi pénzt veszítsen” – az MNB-alapítvány súlyos vagyonvesztésének kulisszatitkai
stories.summary: Az MNB-alapítvány vagyonkezelője, az Optima több százmillió frankos programmal próbált többséget szerezni a svájci luxusingatlanos Ultima Capitalban, de a befektetés végül súlyos pénzügyi teher lett. Az alapítványtól kiperelt dokumentumok segítségével rekonstruáljuk, hogyan olvadt el egy százmilliárdos nagyságrendű befektetés értéke és hogyan került a magyar fél kiszolgáltatott helyzetbe.

stories.title: Toroczkai László megmagyarázta, hogyan értette, hogy Orbán Viktor államférfi
stories.summary: A Mi Hazánk elnöke arról is beszélt: a Fidesz-szavazók részéről érez társadalmi igényt arra, hogy legyen valamiféle ellenzéki együttműködés a Mi Hazánk és a Fidesz között.

stories.title: Ez kevés lesz, mint Hatvanpusztán a magánpénz – mondta Magyar Péter az ellenzékének a parlamentben
stories.summary: Magyar Péter arról beszélt napirend előtt, hogy a Fidesz és a KDNP cserben hagyta a választóit, amikor az Alaptörvény módosítása miatt bojkottálták a parlament hétfői ülését. A KDNP szerint Magyar Péter nem kormányfőként, hanem az ellenzék ellenzékeként viselkedik.

stories.title: A magyar ipar utolsó bástyája egy szervergyártó cég
stories.summary: A komáromi Foxconn gyakorlatilag egymagában húzta fel a májusi ipari termelést.

stories.title: Hungarian Parliament passes law ousting Orbán-allied president
stories.summary: President Sulyok has five days to sign the amendment, which also sends several judges of the Constitutional Court, including its Orbán-loyalist president to retirement.

stories.title: Van esélye a hazai cégeknek az uniós pénzeső alá állni?
stories.summary: Nagyon rövid határidővel jelentek meg a sok százmilliárdos hálózatfejlesztési és okosmérős pályázatok, így előfordulhat, hogy az érdemi magyar részvétel a csavarhúzós alvállalkozókra korlátozódik.

stories.title: Kecskemét fideszes polgármestere szerint Magyar Péter kérte arra, ne menjen el a Mercedes gyáravatójára
stories.summary: Ő tiszteletben tartotta a kérést, ezért nem volt jelen az eseményen.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor Magyar Péter fotójával eltemette a demokratikus Magyarországot
stories.summary: Magyar szerint viszont Orbán hagyta magára a közösségét, amikor egy focimeccsen „küzd az önkény ellen”.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor épp kiírja magát a magyar politikából
stories.summary: A bukott miniszterelnök most válik bukott politikussá: telekürtöli az országot, hogy a Tisza megdönti a magyar demokráciát, ő meg elrepül Amerikába focimeccset nézni. Fideszes legyen a talpán, aki ezután kitart mellette.

stories.title: New York Times: A Moszad Budapesten akarta beszervezni a volt iráni elnököt
stories.summary: A lap forrásai szerint ez volt a célja annak a 2024-es, nagy port kavart esetnek, amikor Mahmúd Ahmadinezsádot meghívták a Nemzeti Közszolgálati Egyetemre előadást tartani.

stories.title: Orbán János Dénes reagált a KMTG-től kapott több száz milliós kifizetésekre
stories.summary: A jogokat a KMTG Orbán János Dénestől mint magánszemélytől vásárolta meg, miközben Orbán János Dénes a szervezet szakmai vezetője.

stories.title: Róna Dániel: Orbán olyan helyzetben van, amiből én nem látom a kiutat
stories.summary: A 21 Kutatóközpont vezetője szerint a Fidesz nagyon nem találta el a #StopÖnkény! kampányt, és fényévekre van attól, hogy más szavazókat is megszólítson. A Tisza vonata tovább robog, viszont Magyar Péter elszámolta magát.

stories.title: Megszavazták az Alaptörvény-módosítást, amely megszünteti Sulyok Tamás és Polt Péter mandátumát
stories.summary: A Fidesz–KDNP bojkottálta a szavazást, Gulyás Gergely lemondott a frakcióvezetői tisztségéről. A Mi Hazánk nemmel szavazott, a tiszások felállva tapsoltak. Magyar Péter szerint ezzel a módosítással lezárják a fideszes rombolás korszakát. A következő országgyűlési választáson nem indulhat az, aki legalább 12 évig volt képviselő, a vármegyéket visszanevezték megyékre, és megszűnik az Országgyűlési Őrség.

stories.title: Csak októberben tárgyal Sulyok Tamás indítványáról a Velencei Bizottság
stories.summary: A Sándor-palota közlése szerint Sulyok álláspontja változatlan abban, hogy az alkotmánymódosítás előtt be kellett volna várni a Velencei Bizottság véleményét.

stories.title: Magyar Péter szerint az „amerikai emigrációba kényszerült” Orbán legelsőként hagyta el a süllyedő hajót
stories.summary: Sajtótájékoztatót tartott Magyar Péter az Alaptörvény módosításának szavazása után.

stories.title: Gulyás Gergely már pénteken szólt Orbánnak, hogy távozik a frakció éléről, szerinte az utódja jobban fogja végezni a feladatot
stories.summary: Az ATV-nek adott interjúban nem nevezte meg, kit szeretne utódjának.

stories.title: Egy héten belül a második halálos kimenetelű lövöldözésben vettek részt az ICE ügynökei
stories.summary: Egy helyi jogvédő szervezet szerint egy 26 éves kolumbiai férfi az áldozat, aki rendelkezett munkavállalási engedéllyel az Egyesült Államokban.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor: A jogtalanul pozícióba ültetett új elnök nem lehet törvényes, így a döntései sem lesznek azok
stories.summary: A volt miniszterelnök a foci vébéről azt üzente: „Magyarországnak joga van ellenállni. És meg is fogjuk tenni!”

stories.title: Von der Leyen: Egyetértés van, hogy kell egy korhatár a közösségi médiához csatlakozásra
stories.summary: Az Európai Bizottság még a nyáron bemutatja a javaslatát egy szakértői jelentés alapján, amely szerint 13 éves korig csak felügyelet mellett szabad bevezetni a közösségi médiát.

stories.title: Was Erdoğan's gun gift a faux pas or an old-school diplomatic tradition?
stories.summary: When NATO leaders opened their gift boxes from the Turkish president and found a .357 Magnum revolver, some panicked and others laughed. But gifting heads of state lavish, personalised firearms is a tradition as old as Samuel Colt — and as recent as a Czech pistol presented to Trump in 2019.

stories.title: Hungary public media reform begins with black screen apology
stories.summary: People tuning in to public service media in Hungary on Tuesday were greeted by a black screen and an apology. Peter Magyar has begun dismantling one of Viktor Orban's most potent tools of power.

stories.title: Poland could cut arms aid to Ukraine as WWII spat continues
stories.summary: As Poland observes a day of remembrance for the victims of a Ukrainian nationalist military unit, there are increasing signs that it could cut off its neighbor from military aid, even as the war against Russia continues.

stories.title: ‘We are dying little by little here’: asylum seekers at mercy of Home Office hotel closures
stories.summary: Legal challenges launched over accommodation ‘adequacy’ as UK government closes more asylum hotels Huda and her two children aged 10 and 12 had been living in two rooms in a London hotel for six months when they were told with just a few days’ notice they would be moved. The 41-year-old engineering graduate from Tunisia fled death threats from extended family and is waiting for an asylum application to be processed. The Home Office had decided that Staycity, the hotel the family was staying in,

stories.title: Europeans to partner with Kyiv on homegrown missile defense
stories.summary: PARIS — Nine European countries on Monday agreed to back an ambitious Ukrainian effort to develop a domestic missile defense system that, if successful, would reduce reliance on U.S. technology. Ukraine depends on the United States for PAC-2 and PAC-3 Patriot interceptors to take down incoming Russian missiles. But the U.S. and its allies fired hundreds of them in the early phase of the war against Iran, straining supplies. During recent Russian attacks, Ukraine has run dangerously low on interc

stories.title: Graham death leaves Israel with a DC void
stories.summary: Sen. Lindsey Graham’s death is a major blow to Israel’s efforts to persuade the U.S. to keep up its military campaign against Iran, as well as to other Israeli goals in Washington, according to those familiar with U.S. strategy in the region. The 71-year-old Republican from South Carolina was long one of Israel’s biggest champions. He backed the Middle Eastern nation on numerous fronts, from securing arms deals with the U.S. to trying to normalize relations with the Saudis. Above all, Graha

stories.title: US launches third night of strikes on Iran as Trump announces Hormuz blockade
stories.summary: American president says ships will be charged for safe passage through strait in apparent policy reversal Middle East crisis live – latest updates The US has launched its third consecutive night of strikes on Iran hours after Donald Trump said Washington would reinstate a maritime blockade on the country and, in an apparently policy reversal, charge ships for safe passage. “These strikes will continue imposing a heavy cost on Iranian forces and degrade their ability to attack innocent civilians

stories.title: Japan admits growing need to counter espionage after Russian ‘den of spies’ report
stories.summary: Issue must be addressed with ‘even greater rigour’, says government spokesman, after New York Times report on how it has become a spy hub for Vladimir Putin Japan has said it recognised the need to counter foreign intelligence better after the New York Times reported that Russia had turned the country into a “den of spies” and key source of weapons components. The newspaper, in an investigation published on Sunday, reported that thanks to “weak espionage laws”, Moscow was using Japan as a key hu

stories.title: Marco Rubio launches campaign to dismantle international criminal court
stories.summary: Trump’s secretary of state claims the global tribunal is interfering with US military and law enforcement operations Marco Rubio, the US secretary of state, launched a campaign to dismantle the international criminal court (ICC) on Monday, claiming the global tribunal was interfering with US military and law enforcement operations at the risk of American sovereignty. Rubio invoked images of US border patrol agents and elected leaders being “dragged before an international court” and tried by jud

stories.title: Sweden prides itself on equality – so why is its political gender gap growing?
stories.summary: As general election looms, survey shows twice as many men as women support far-right Sweden Democrats One is led by Sweden’s first female prime minister, Magdalena Andersson , and has promised smaller school-class sizes, more housing and free dental care for young people. The other, led by Jimmie Åkesson , has neo-Nazi roots and has pledged to lower taxes, improve public safety and treat “anti-Swedishness” as a hate crime. In the run-up to Sweden’s general election in September, the Social Democ

stories.title: North Sea oil industry urges Burnham to approve new drilling in UK waters
stories.summary: Lobby appeals to prospective PM’s reindustrialisation agenda as it pushes for Rosebank and Jackdaw approval The UK’s North Sea oil industry has made a last-ditch attempt to curry favour with the Labour government by appealing to Andy Burnham’s reindustrialisation agenda just days before he is expected to become Britain’s next prime minister. Industry lobbyists have written to more than 400 Labour MPs to call on the government’s new leaders to allow more oil and gas drilling in UK waters to suppo

stories.title: US refunds $81bn in Trump tariffs after supreme court ruled them illegal
stories.summary: Government has been forced to pay back duties to companies that imported goods into the US that were hit by Trump’s tariffs Business live – latest updates The US government has already paid back tens of billions of dollars in tariffs it collected before the supreme court ruled them illegal, according to budget figures released on Monday. Tariffs – taxes on imported goods – have been a key part of Donald Trump’s economic plan since he took office again last year. Continue reading...

stories.title: US strikes Iran for third consecutive night as Trump plans Hormuz transit fee – Middle East crisis live
stories.summary: UAE says Iranian cruise missiles hit two oil tankers in strait, killing a crew member and wounding eight US launches third night of strikes on Iran as Trump announces Hormuz blockade Resurgent oil and fuel prices could cement a fourth interest rate rise in Australia this year if Donald Trump’s renewed conflict with Iran is not resolved within a week , economists warn. US missile strikes on Iran and Trump’s announcement of a new maritime blockade has lifted oil prices to their highest point in th

stories.title: Bastille Day: France to hold parade with Ukraine in focus
stories.summary: The celebrations will feature troops and warplanes from around Europe in a show of support for Ukraine, as it fends off Russia's ongoing invasion. It is Emmanuel Macron's last Bastille Day as France's president.

stories.title: Farage’s strategy for the Burnham era? It’s all about him
stories.summary: LONDON — Nigel Farage is spending the summer railing against “the Establishment.” The Reform UK leader faces two tougher-than-expected by-elections amid mounting scrutiny of his finances — and is now fending off criticism of his response to the killing of Ann Widdecombe, a former Conservative minister who became a high-profile Reform spokesperson. It is just the latest row in which Farage’s party — still leading in British opinion polls as the governing Labour Party prepares to make Andy B

stories.title: Mexico to file criminal complaints over migrants killed by ICE in US
stories.summary: Claudia Sheinbaum says Mexicans ‘outraged’ over killing last week of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo by agents in Houston Claudia Sheinbaum announced on Monday that Mexico would be filing criminal complaints in the US for the deaths of more than a dozen Mexican migrants in immigration detention and those killed in anti-migrant operations. The deaths include last week’s killing of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo in Houston, whom Sheinbaum said was “practically murdered”. Continue reading...

stories.title: ICE fatally shoots 26-year-old Colombian man in Maine during immigration operation – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. Sign up for the Breaking News US email The Democrat’s outgoing senator for Michigan Gary Peters has endorsed member of Congress Haley Stevens to be his successor over Abdul El-Sayed in the state’s neck-and-neck primary race set for 4 August. “She has demonstrated to me time and time again that she’s a fighter,” Peters told the Detroit News . “We need workhorses in the Senate, and we need someone who can do that job from day one. This is not a place for on-the-job tr

stories.title: Trump says US reinstating blockade on Iranian ports – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is closed – our live coverage continues here Bahrain’s military has accused Iran of targeting civilians with its latest attacks on the country, after Tehran said it had struck US military facilities and infrastructure there earlier. “Iran continues its systematic hostile approach through its heinous attacks with missiles and drones that target civilians in the Kingdom of Bahrain,” the general command of Bahrain’s military said, adding that air defences “intercepted and destroyed a numb

stories.title: ‘This process has turned into a form of torture’: inside the trial of Erdoğan’s challenger
stories.summary: He was elected mayor of Istanbul in 2019, and had announced his candidacy for the 2028 presidential elections. But Ekrem İmamoğlu is now behind bars, and his trial, on charges including fraud and organised crime, could take 12 years This piece first appeared in the Dial There’s a Turkish saying, “ Silivri soğuktur ”: Silivri is cold. You’ll hear it from journalists, politicians and activists after they say something critical of President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan’s government. The kind of comments th

stories.title: LGBTQ+ pop-up in former Sydney church ordered by landlord to cancel events after religious protest
stories.summary: Landlord of deconsecrated building sends notice of breach to Divine Playhouse, claiming it ‘insulted and mocked’ beliefs of Christian Australians Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A deconsecrated Sydney church newly transformed into an LGBTQ+ venue has had to cancel events and been ordered to stop “engaging in offensive trade” by its landlord after religious groups protested against its opening night. Divine Playho

stories.title: Universities not doing enough to deal with protests and ‘highly personal’ attacks, antisemitism royal commission hears
stories.summary: Royal commission into antisemitism hears professor whose office was occupied by protesters did not know at time whether it was a ‘terrorist attack’ Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A Jewish professor has told the royal commission into antisemitism the University of Melbourne needs to show it doesn’t “tolerate misbehaviour” after the expulsion of two pro-Palestine student activists who occupied his office was overt

stories.title: South East Water to pay £30.5m penalty after multiple supply failures
stories.summary: Ofwat says repeated errors led to ‘real disruption and hardship for residents and businesses across many years’ Business live – latest updates South East Water will pay £30.5m after a series of supply interruptions, customer failings and for breaching its licence, the regulator Ofwat has said. The watchdog said the redress package concluded three investigations into the supplier and included a previously proposed £22m fine for water supply failures between 2020 and 2023 affecting more than 286,0

stories.title: Police claim breakthrough in Melbourne’s hospitality wars, alleging links to overseas crime figure
stories.summary: Victoria police say they have arrested ‘high-ranking’ man from syndicate accused of orchestrating arson attacks Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Police have arrested a man they say orchestrated a wave of Melbourne arson attacks on behalf of a crime syndicate based in the Middle East, marking a major development in the city’s escalating hospitality wars. Detectives claim the arrest is the most significant since an

stories.title: France hopes to send ‘strategic signal’ about Europe’s renewed military focus at Bastille Day celebrations – Europe live
stories.summary: Many leaders from the ‘Coalition of the Willing’ stayed overnight in Paris to attend the parade, which will be Emmanuel Macron’s last as president You can watch our live stream of the Bastille Day parade in Paris here, with final preparations now under way before the event starts soon. Most leaders are already in their premium seats, including French PM Sébastien Lecornu, with France’s Emmanuel Macron expected shortly before the start at 10am (9am UK). Continue reading...

stories.title: India news: Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike tests Modi
stories.summary: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuks has been fasting in protest of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan since over two weeks. As his health deteriorates, the pressure piles up on Prime Minister Narendra Modi. DW has more.

stories.title: Wildfire raging south of Paris could have been set deliberately, says minister
stories.summary: The interior minister has said the fire near Paris could have been set deliberately and two people have been arrested.

stories.title: Health fears as frail Indian activist on hunger strike loses 8.2kg
stories.summary: The environmental activist has been on a hunger strike for 16 days and is getting visibly weaker.

stories.title: US launches strikes on Iran for third straight night as Trump says deal still 'possible'
stories.summary: Following the latest US strikes, the UAE said Iran attacked two ships in the Strait of Hormuz with missiles, as Iran said it carried out strikes on Bahrain.

stories.title: Death toll from Bangkok bar fire rises to 30 as police probe safety lapses
stories.summary: Survivors and first responders reported locked doors and a lack of signage marking emergency exits.

stories.title: Trump watches IndyCar pit stop at the White House
stories.summary: President Donald Trump watched an IndyCar pit stop demonstration at the White House as he promoted the inaugural Freedom 250 Grand Prix, a race set to take place around Washington's National Mall next month.

stories.title: China's June exports surge 27% from a year earlier as AI boom drives strong demand
stories.summary: China’s exports accelerated in June, jumping 27% from a year earlier thanks partly to the boom in artificial intelligence, the customs agency said on Tuesday.

stories.title: US releases video of sea drone strike on Iranian naval base
stories.summary: The US military has released video it says shows three Corsair unmanned surface vessels striking a ship maintenance facility and submarine at Iran's Bandar Abbas Naval Base on 12 July. US Central Command says it was the first combat use of American sea drones.

stories.title: Russia sanctions package pushed toward midweek deadline
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The EU’s foreign ministers failed to sign off on the bloc’s 21st package of sanctions against Russia on Monday, and ambassadors will now convene to discuss freezing a price cap on Russian crude exports by Wednesday — the last day before it resets. The EU’s top diplomat Kaja Kallas confirmed the setback on Monday afternoon: “Yes, I also regret that we don’t have agreement on the 21st package,” she said. The package includes keeping the oil price cap at $44.10 per barrel. Without freezi

stories.title: EU capitals fail to force Commission’s hand on Israeli settler sanctions
stories.summary: The European Union will not yet introduce a ban on products from territories illegally occupied by Israel after less than half of the bloc’s foreign ministers voted for action on the issue during a tense meeting Monday. A group of EU countries has been urging the executive to ban trade with Israeli settlements in the occupied West Bank, in what has become a battle between the EU’s institutions as well as between capitals with differing stances on Israel’s actions in the occupie

stories.title: Sale of multimillion-dollar T rex skeleton is big headache for scientists
stories.summary: Palaeontologists warn before auction at Sotheby’s in New York that super-rich collectors are harming research With its dagger-like teeth, bone-crushing bite and behemothic size, the fearsome Tyrannosaurus rex ruled western North America during the late Cretaceous period. Now its fossilised remains are about to dominate the auction house, with a price tag to terrify punters. On Tuesday, one of the largest and most complete T rex skeletons discovered to date is to be auctioned by Sotheby’s in New

stories.title: UK’s alcohol-free beer boom threatened by regulations, trade body warns
stories.summary: BBPA wants content definition for beer to be considered alcohol free to be changed from 0.05% to 0.5% Pubs and brewers are being prevented from capitalising on Britons’ record-breaking thirst for alcohol-free beer because of over-strict regulation, a trade body has warned. More than 64m pints of low- and no-alcohol beer is forecast to be sold over the summer, an increase of 8m compared with 2025, the British Beer and Pub Association (BBPA) said, citing the figures as proof that the category is “

stories.title: Bangkok bar fire: death toll reaches 30 as police investigate negligence as ‘primary theory’
stories.summary: Bar owner in Thailand offers ‘deepest apologies for this tragic incident’ as police investigate whether exits were either blocked or hard to access The Bangkok pub that has become the scene of the city’s deadliest blaze in 17 years has said it will cooperate with an investigation into alleged negligence, as the death toll rose to 30. The local district office said on Tuesday that three more people died after the devastating fire that broke out in the early hours of Monday. An initial assessment

stories.title: Hanson’s meeting with UK far-right activist will ‘play out poorly’, Coalition MP says – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Ed Husic says weakening copyright to benefit AI companies would betray Labor party’s ethos Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast George Miller remembers Sam Neill as ‘comprehensively an amazing person’ Acclaimed director George Miller shared memories of the late Sam Neill , who died yesterday at the age of 78. He was such an exemplary human being. We saw him as an actor and we got to know him as an actor. The world did. But what was so amazing about

stories.title: Teenager left with life-threatening injuries after alleged stabbing at Brisbane school
stories.summary: Boy, 16, charged after an alleged altercation with a 17-year-old boy at Islamic College of Brisbane Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Police have charged a 16-year-old boy with stabbing a 17-year-old boy at a Brisbane high school, allegedly leaving him with life-threatening injuries. The alleged incident took place at the Islamic College of Brisbane in Karawatha, south of Brisbane, at about 11.45am on Tuesday. Cont

stories.summary: US political leaders must be more clear-eyed about our global alliances, without embracing his scorched-earth approach Donald Trump memorably took out a full-page advertisement in multiple newspapers in 1987 charging that America was carrying too much weight for its allies. stories.summary: Robust action is needed to protect European industries from unfair competition. The alternative is social strife amid growing insecurity According to a recent analysis , China enjoys a surplus in its manufactured goods trade with the European Union that is roughly equivalent to Italy’s national income. stories.title: Firefighting planes scrambled from south of France to tackle huge wildfire near Paris
stories.summary: Officials say blaze in Fontainebleau forest is of ‘exceptional scale’, with 900 homes evacuated and road and rail links hit French firefighters are tackling a blaze of unprecedented scale sweeping through Fontainebleau forest south-east of Paris, while in southern Spain the prime minister visited the scene of a deadly wildfire and warned: “The climate emergency kills.” The fire in Fontainebleau, a one-time royal hunting preserve about 40 miles (60km) from the French capital that today is dotted

stories.title: Trump renews Iran blockade and again threatens to take control of strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: US president declares waterway open and demands tariff as both sides engage in heavy drone and missile exchanges Middle East crisis live – latest updates Donald Trump has once again threatened to take control of the strait of Hormuz, as he announced the reimposition of a naval blockade on Iran and demanded a 20% tariff on all cargoes shipped through the key maritime passage. Declaring the strait “open”, Trump suggested in a post on his Truth Social platform that the US should be known henceforth

stories.title: ‘Coalition of the willing’ to build shared European anti-ballistic programme
stories.summary: Ukraine and nine other countries including UK issue joint statement as leaders meet Volodymyr Zelenskyy in Paris Ukraine and nine other countries including the UK, Germany and France are to build a shared protection programme for Europe against ballistic missiles, using Kyiv’s experience in fighting Russia’s full-scale invasion for more than four years. “Our goal is to build a shared ballistic missile defence capability for Europe,” the 10 nations said in a statement on Monday as leaders met the

stories.title: Leaders give press conference after ‘coalition of the willing’ meeting in Paris – as it happened
stories.summary: French president Emmanuel Macron has been hosting leaders amid hopes that Ukraine’s recent advances could force Putin towards negotiations in Kyiv Meanwhile, Russia has been forced to suspend shipping in the Sea of Azov after 90 vessels were targeted by Ukrainian drones in less than a week. Continue reading...

stories.title: US launches more strikes on Iran as renewed standoff over Strait of Hormuz intensifies
stories.summary: Iranian state media reported US strikes targeting large areas across southern and western Iran, including Qeshm island and Bandar Abbas near the strait, and in Khuzestan province bordering Iraq. Missile alert sirens also sounded at dawn Monday in Bahrain, home to the US Navy's 5th Fleet.

stories.title: Iran war: US continues strikes as Trump reinstates blockade
stories.summary: The US has carried out a third consecutive night of strikes on Iran. Donald Trump says Washington is reinstating a naval blockade and warns Tehran will be “hit hard” as tensions continue to escalate. DW has more.

stories.title: US: 12 states challenge Paramount's buyout of Warner
stories.summary: The states have filed a lawsuit arguing that a merger of Hollywood giant studios Paramount and Warner would "extinguish competition" and increase consumer prices.

stories.title: Death toll in Bangkok bar fire reaches 30
stories.summary: As Bangkok kicks-off an investigation into a fire in a bar last Sunday, three people among the wounded have died. The number of deaths from the blaze now stand at 30.

stories.title: Five years after deadly floods, a German valley prepares for the next one
stories.summary: New walls, redesigned bridges and restored floodplains: towns in a small German valley are testing how they can survive the next extreme flood.

stories.title: Renewed US-Iran war is hitting Gulf countries hard
stories.summary: After US strikes on Iran, Iran has targeted US allies in the Middle East. Even if Iran isn't targeting the governments or populations of its neighbors, Gulf countries have much to lose as fighting escalates.

stories.title: Ukraine, 9 countries form ballistic missile defense coalition
stories.summary: Ukraine and nine European countries launched a coalition to protect Europe from ballistic missiles, drawing on Kyiv’s wartime experience.

stories.title: World Cup: Balogun red and expansion keep FIFA in spotlight
stories.summary: The fallout from Folarin Balogun's red card reversal continues as reports suggest the chairman of FIFA’s disciplinary committee made the decision unilaterally. Meanwhile, the World Cup could be set to expand once more.

stories.title: South Korea airs concerns over North Korean border activity
stories.summary: South Korea has voiced concern that its neighbor's actions along the demilitarized zone between the two countries may be in breach of the 1953 armistice agreement.

stories.title: Lindsey Graham's sister chosen as replacement after senator's death
stories.summary: The South Carolina governor chose the senator's little sister to serve out the rest of his term after Graham's death on Saturday.

stories.title: 'Hero, legend, sweetheart': Tributes to Jurassic Park actor Sam Neill, who has died aged 78
stories.summary: Friends, politicians and fellow film stars remember Neill following his "sudden and unexpected" death.

stories.title: Identity of Sydney daycare worker facing 329 child abuse offences revealed
stories.summary: Hamish Tait is accused of abusing 136 children at five childcare facilities over a 16-year period.

stories.title: Colombian national killed by ICE agent during operation in Maine
stories.summary: It comes less than a week after an undocumented migrant was fatally shot in Houston by an immigration agent.

stories.title: Australian police reveal unseen photos 25 years after British backpacker murder
stories.summary: Police hope the images could jog memories that will help them find the body of Peter Falconio.

stories.title: US burrito giant Chipotle opening first outlet in Mexico
stories.summary: Other US fast food brands have struggled to succeed in the countries their dishes originated.

stories.title: Marco Rubio launches sweeping campaign to 'dismantle' ICC 'threat to US sovereignty'
stories.summary: The Trump administration said it would pressure other countries to withdraw from the court, marking a sharp escalation in the US effort to isolate the Hague-based institution.

stories.title: How US commerce secretary's Epstein links were uncovered by British whistleblower
stories.summary: Simon Andriesz made the discovery about Howard Lutnick in publicly released Epstein files.

stories.title: Flowers and forensic teams at Bangkok bar where deadly fire killed at least 27
stories.summary: At least 27 people died and around 70 were injured in a bar fire in Bangkok, as police probe blocked exits and faulty wiring in the 50-year-old venue.

stories.title: King’s Swan Upping on the Thames shows healthy cygnets but rising litter
stories.summary: Traditional skiffs glided along the River Thames during Swan Upping as crews counted and weighed cygnets, reporting encouraging numbers while warning about the growing threat of river litter.

stories.title: ICE agent shoots dead Colombian immigrant in Maine
stories.summary: The 26-year-old, who had permission to work in the US, was killed in Biddeford when an officer opened fire, saying his car was a threat. It is the ninth death tied to Trump’s migration crackdown.

stories.title: Sudan's paramilitary RSF chief sentenced to death over war crimes
stories.summary: Mohamed Hamdan Dagalo was convicted in absentia along with 15 RSF commanders and allies.

stories.title: Peace is possible, Aliyev tells global media forum in Shusha
stories.summary: Azerbaijan's president also told the Shusha Global Media Forum that long-term gas contracts were needed for Baku to expand production, and said that Baku was considering leaving the Council of Europe.

stories.title: South Africa says more than 53,000 foreigners deported in migration campaign
stories.summary: South Africa is cracking down on undocumented migrants following widespread anti-immigration protests.

stories.title: Weather tracker: Unusually warm rivers affect French nuclear power plants
stories.summary: High temperatures and below average rainfall put pressure on waterways used to cool reactors Above average temperatures combined with below average rainfall across much of western and central Europe during June and the first half of July have placed increasing pressure on rivers, ecosystems and energy infrastructure. Persistent high pressure brought prolonged sunshine, suppressed rainfall and enhanced evaporation, causing river levels to fall and water temperatures to increase. These unusually w

stories.title: At least 27 killed in fire at Bangkok pub with another 22 critically injured
stories.summary: The fire is one of the deadliest such incidents in the popular tourist destination in ⁠recent years An explosive fire at a popular pub in Thailand’s ⁠capital, Bangkok, has killed 27 ⁠people and left another 22 ​in critical condition, in one of the deadliest such incidents in the tourism hub in recent years. Officials said they were investigating whether emergency exits may have been obstructed, hindering people from escaping the burning Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao pub. Continue reading...

stories.title: Killings continue on Del Monte farm in Kenya, families say, after G4S hired for security
stories.summary: Exclusive: Three men killed in incidents over past year allegedly involving G4S guards, who replaced in-house team after previous deaths Bereaved families and politicians have raised alarm about continued killings on Del Monte’s pineapple farm in Kenya despite the company hiring a British security firm to replace its in-house security team after previous deaths were exposed by the Guardian. The multinational food company appointed G4S to guard the farm, which is estimated to cover at least 40 sq

stories.title: British couple named among 13 killed in Spanish wildfires
stories.summary: Pete and Fran Gillam confirmed dead as authorities use DNA samples to identify victims of blaze in Almería A British couple have been named among the 13 people killed by wildfires in Spain, as authorities race to use DNA to identify victims who were unable to escape the blaze. Pete and Fran Gillam, who lived in Bédar, the village that bore the brunt of the wildfires on Thursday, were confirmed dead by their family. Continue reading...

stories.title: Israel sets October date for first elections since Hamas attacks in 2023
stories.summary: Vote will allow Israelis to pass judgment on Benjamin Netanyahu and his handling of conflicts in Gaza and Iran Middle East crisis live – latest updates Israel will hold national elections on 27 October, giving its citizens their first chance to pass judgment on the prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, and his coalition since the Hamas-led attacks of 7 October 2023. The Knesset, Israel’s parliament, will be dissolved on Friday. With just a few days left in session, the most far-right government in

stories.title: Germany: Iraqi sentenced to life for enslaving Yazidi girls
stories.summary: The man and his former partner are accused of holding two Yazidi girls captive for years while subjecting them to forced labor, abuse and torture before handing them over to other IS members.

stories.title: Germany news: Merz calls for monetary policy dialogue with China
stories.summary: The German chancellor says the EU cannot win against a competitor that artificially manipulates its currency. Meanwhile, another US national suffering from Ebola has landed in Germany for treatment.

stories.title: EU experts urge social media restrictions for kids under 13
stories.summary: An expert panel has recommended limiting access to social media for young children across the EU. The European Commission is expected to present a proposal for legislation on the issue in the coming months.

stories.title: Kenya: What impact will Dangote's new refinery have on the region?
stories.summary: Is Kenya Africa's next major energy hub? A plan from Dangote Industries to build a massive refinery off the country's coast could reshape fuel supply, trade and economic growth across East Africa.

stories.title: Iran: Living in limbo between war and ceasefire
stories.summary: Repeated ceasefire violations, contradictory political signals and ongoing emergency measures have fueled exhaustion, anger and deep uncertainty among many Iranians.

stories.title: Iran war: Trump reinstates US blockade of Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: President Donald Trump says the US will restart its naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, targeting Iranian ships and those doing business with Iran.

stories.title: Germany set to curb its Freedom of Information Act
stories.summary: The German government wants to amend the Freedom of Information Act. Critics fear the changes will water down the government's obligation to disclose information.

stories.title: Gaza Strip: EU conference pledges €900m in donations
stories.summary: The conference in Brussels was attended by Palestinian representatives. The EU's top diplomat said the bloc's support depends on reforms in the Palestinian Authority.

stories.title: Person killed in ICE-involved shooting in Maine
stories.summary: US Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents were involved in a fatal shooting in Biddeford, according to Maine's House speaker. It comes days after ICE allegedly shot a man in Texas during a traffic stop.

stories.title: Azerbaijan considering full withdrawal from Council of Europe, Aliyev says
stories.summary: President Ilham Aliyev said Baku was considering full withdrawal from the 46-member organisation after Azerbaijan's voting rights in PACE were suspended in 2024.

stories.title: People flee Bangkok bar as huge flames shoot out from doorway
stories.summary: At least 28 people have died and 25 are critically injured after a fire tore through a bar in a popular Bangkok district.

stories.title: Men disguised as police kill Ecuadorean drug lord's brother
stories.summary: The victim, David Macías, was the brother of drug lord 'El Fito', who is awaiting trial in the US.

stories.title: Drone footage shows devastation after deadly Spain wildfire

stories.title: German court convicts Iraqi couple of enslaving Yazidi girls
stories.summary: The pair, named only as Twana H.S. and Asia R. A., left Germany for Iraq in 2015 and became members of the Islamic State group.

stories.title: The SpaceX IPO made history. One month on has it lost momentum?
stories.summary: A month on from the firm's stock market debut, the reality of how SpaceX currently makes money has seemed to come into clearer focus.

stories.title: A versatile actor whose roles went far beyond Jurassic Park - Sam Neill dies aged 78
stories.summary: The actor built up a long string of screen credits over a five-decade career.

stories.title: Video shows civilians taking cover during Russian strike on Sumy

stories.title: At least 28 killed, 25 critically injured after fire engulfs Bangkok bar

stories.title: UK-Switzerland deal to scrap roaming charges and allow Britons to use e-gates

stories.title: Volkswagen planning to cut up to 100,000 jobs globally
stories.summary: The group, which includes Porsche and Audi, has faced a steep fall in profits and tough competition from China.

stories.title: Watch: Horses evacuated as fire approaches riding centre near Paris

stories.title: Trump reimposes US blockade and demands 20% Hormuz shipping fee

stories.title: Bison rams grandfather in Yellowstone Park, knocking him metres into the air
stories.summary: The man and his grandson appeared a safe distance away from the bison before it quickly chased them down.

stories.title: Briton tells of surviving Spain wildfire in car as wife and friends died trying to run to safety
stories.summary: Malcolm Timbrell's wife and their friends are thought to have died when a devastating wildfire tore through their village in Spain.

stories.title: US judge voids Donald Trump's $1.8bn settlement with IRS that gave him immunity from tax audits

stories.summary: Összesen 17,5 milliárd forint ment volna a Lounge Event Kft.-nek a programsorozatra, végül az összeg kevesebb mint egyharmadából rendezi meg a Tisza-kormány az idei ünnepséget.

It is a record pace to set up and start this kind of research, scientists said, with pati

But in recent months, they discovered another alarming development: wildlife rapidly infected with the new world screwworm. stories.summary: 50 helyszínen végeztek házkutatást, a hatóság két embert őrizetbe vett, és 130 raklap banánt foglalt le.

stories.summary: Egy nő nemrég komoly kifogásokat fogalmazott meg férje ellátásával kapcsolatban. Az Okfő azt ígérte, mindent megtesznek azért, hogy hasonló eset ne forduljon elő többet.

stories.title: Négy út áll Sulyok Tamás előtt az Alaptörvény módosítása után
stories.summary: Öt napja van átgondolni, hogy aláírja-e a módosítást. Egy újabb alkotmánybírósági körrel még tudja húzni az időt, a Tisza Párt a megfosztási eljárás elindításával azonban akár már jövő keddre megszüntetheti a megbízatását. Reagált a Sándor-palota a Telexnek a szavazás után.

stories.title: Forsthoffer Ágnes már alá is írta az Alaptörvény-módosítást, Sulyoknak öt napja maradt dönteni
stories.summary: Az Országgyűlés elnöke villámgyorsan elküldte a megszavazott indítványt a köztársasági elnöknek.

stories.title: Eldőlt, Andy Burnham volt manchesteri polgármester lesz a következő brit miniszterelnök
stories.summary: A Munkáspárt 349 képviselője jelölte őt Keir Starmer utódjaként, ezzel pedig másnak már matematikai esélye sincs, hogy összegyűjtse az induláshoz szükséges jelölést.

stories.title: Négykarú octopus és tetőről lógó autók alatt jártuk be az új kecskeméti Mercedes-gyárat, ahonnan évente több tízezer autót exportálnak majd
stories.summary: Hatalmas gépek, alvázra szerelt akkumulátorok és önvezető robotok fogadtak minket a hétfőn átadott üzemben. Az itt gyártott autókat egy kivételével a világ minden országába szállítják majd, jövőre pedig ide jöhet majd a G-Mercik kisebb változata is.

stories.title: How a Jewish heir is taking on Germany's far-right AfD
stories.summary: The AfD is using the iconic GDR-era Simson moped in its campaign. Now, a Jewish heir to the founding family is fighting to protect the company's legacy.

stories.title: Germany news: Neuschwanstein killer fights extradition to US
stories.summary: A man who murdered a woman at Neuschwanstein Castle says he fears execution in the US. Germany has been hit by another heat wave, though no records are likely to be broken this time, forecasters say.

stories.title: Germany's Ruhr Valley: Cities on brink of financial collapse
stories.summary: With tax revenues down and social spending up, more and more cities and towns in the Ruhrgebiet are struggling. Oberhausen is particularly deep in the red.

stories.title: California's largest lake is turning into dust
stories.summary: The Salton Sea is drying up, worsening air pollution for nearby communities. Officials are racing to contain it, but for some families, it's already too late.

stories.title: Are German companies leaving the country?
stories.summary: Should I stay or should I go? Given Germany's high cost and sluggish growth, some business leaders are mulling relocating, or at least investing abroad. DW has a look at the numbers.

stories.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy swaps out prime minister in reshuffle
stories.summary: Ukraine's president announced a government shake up, with PM Yuliia Svyrydenko stepping down after a year in office.

stories.title: Sinner beats Zverev to retain Wimbledon title
stories.summary: Jannik Sinner retained his Wimbledon title by beating Alexander Zverev in Sunday's men's final. The Italian claims his fifth Grand Slam crown and extends his dominance over the German to 10 straight victories.

stories.title: EU approves new passenger rights for air travel
stories.summary: EU ministers have approved a set of reforms giving more rights to air travelers. Among other things, compensation claims are to be made easier.

stories.title: Argentina fans erupt as Messi's men reach World Cup semifinal
stories.summary: Argentina fans celebrate in Buenos Aires after a dramatic 3-1 extra-time win over ten-man Switzerland sends the defending champions into the World Cup semifinals.

stories.title: Jude Bellingham double sends jubilant England into World Cup semis
stories.summary: England beat Norway 2-1 in extra time on 12 July 2026 to reach the World Cup semifinals. Fans celebrated in London after a Jude Bellingham double sent the Three Lions through to face Argentina.

stories.title: 'This landscape is completely charred': Inside the village at epicentre of Spain's wildfires
stories.summary: The BBC's Nick Beake sees homes razed to the ground and "a sea of black" in Bédar.

stories.title: France: hundreds dive into the Seine for Paris’ first river race in 80 years
stories.summary: Swimmers plunged into the Seine near the Eiffel Tower for the Open Swim Harmonie Mutuelle, the first major organised race on the river in nearly 80 years.

stories.title: Israel to hold elections on 27 October in what is seen as referendum on Netanyahu leadership
stories.summary: Israel will hold national elections on 27 October, the last date allowed by law to hold national elections. The Knesset is set to end its current term on 17 July.

stories.title: Spain wildfire: Firefighters gain ground as Almería evacuees return home
stories.summary: Firefighters gained the upper hand on the deadly Almería wildfire after days of battling the flames, as the blaze that killed 12 people and burned 66 square kilometres began to ease, allowing some of the nearly 1,500 evacuees to return home.

stories.title: New era for Gibraltar with removal of 118-year-old border controls with Spain
stories.summary: It is hoped that free movement between the UK territory and Spain will provide an economic boost.

stories.title: The Chinese graduate accused of being Mexico's 'fentanyl king'
stories.summary: Cartel members and ex-colleagues describe how Zhang Zhidong allegedly linked Chinese chemicals to Mexican drug labs.

stories.title: 10 years of Pokémon Go and the millions still trying to catch 'em all
stories.summary: Michael Steranka, vice president at the mobile game's owner Scopely, says it has always been about bringing people together.

stories.title: How Indian scientists are mapping the brain's last frontier
stories.summary: India's new brainstem atlas offers scientists an unprecedented map of one of the brain's least known regions.

stories.title: Watch: At the scene where Bangkok's deadly bar fire unfolded
stories.summary: The BBC’s South East Asia correspondent visits the site where at least 27 people were killed during a bar fire in Thailand.

stories.title: Fire at pub on outskirts of Bangkok kills at least 30 people and injures dozens
stories.summary: The venue has a stage, two bars and a kitchen, with a total capacity of more than 300 people, as well as four fire exits, according to a Facebook post promoting a live show this month.

stories.title: Heavy rains and flash floods in Bangladesh leave 51 dead
stories.summary: Thousands have lost their homes as flash floods and landslides have hit large parts of the country.

stories.title: Major wildfire rages south of Paris during heatwave
stories.summary: A major wildfire has spread through France's Fontainebleau forest south of Paris, prompting a large emergency response, disrupting road and rail traffic, and forcing authorities to deploy firefighting aircraft.

stories.title: Venezuela quake death toll rises to 4,490, officials say, but no number given for missing
stories.summary: Camps for families left homeless have sprung up in stadiums, plazas and on sidewalks in La Guaira as well as nearby Caracas.

stories.title: Nigeria says army has killed 300 bandits in north-western state of Zamfara
stories.summary: Vigilantes also took part in the fight that raged all night and the following morning, residents say Nigerian soldiers killed more than 300 members of kidnapping and cattle bandit gangs in the north-western state of Zamfara this week, according to a government official. Government troops targeted the gangs in Gummi district in a two-day operation that “led to the elimination of more than 300 terrorists”, Zamfara’s information commissioner, Mahmud Muhammad Dantawasa, said in a statement. Continue

stories.title: Toronto shooting: two dead and four injured at Salsa on St Clair street festival
stories.summary: Police say two people exchanged gunfire in shooting that mayor called an ‘irresponsible act of violence’ in festival attended by families A shooting near a Toronto street festival killed two men and wounded four other people on Saturday evening, police said, adding that what initially prompted an active-shooter warning was an exchange of gunfire between two people targeting each other. Toronto police deputy chief Frank Barredo said investigators recovered two firearms after the shooting, which w

stories.title: A revolution in ruins: fury amid the rubble of a housing project in quake-hit Venezuela
stories.summary: Discontent with Trump-backed government mounts as Chávez heirs struggle to respond to disaster for which they seem ill-prepared Even before two powerful earthquakes reduced the OPPE 25 government housing project to an anarchy of shattered concrete and broken lives, the foundations of Hugo Chávez’s populist “Bolivarian” revolution were shaking in what was once a hotbed of support. Gabriel González remembers his elation when, in 2013, he received the keys to his freshly completed apartment in one

stories.title: Germany: Left-wing group says it is behind railway sabotage
stories.summary: The "Angry Birds Commando" group said they placed incendiary devices on a railroad between the two busy cities of Cologne and Düsseldorf. The group blamed industrial technology for the destruction of the environment.

stories.title: War in Ukraine: Kyiv hit by Russian ballistic missiles and drone swarm, 10 injured
stories.summary: Ukraine woke to fresh destruction on 11 July after Russia launched overnight strikes on Kyiv, leaving at least 10 people injured, including a child, according to Ukrainian officials.

stories.title: US Senator Lindsey Graham dies after 'brief and sudden illness'
stories.summary: Graham, a prominent Republican voice on foreign policy, had recently returned from Ukraine, where he met with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy in Kyiv.

stories.title: Venezuela quake death toll passes 4,300 as scale of recovery effort looms large
stories.summary: Nearly 17,000 injured and thousands more listed as missing amid calls by president Delcy Rodríguez and UN for financial help The death toll in Venezuela’s devastating twin earthquakes has passed 4,300, the government said on Saturday. At least 4,333 people were killed and 16,740 injured in the back-to-back quakes on 24 June that flattened entire districts in the coastal state of La Guaira, the Venezuelan parliament chief, Jorge Rodríguez, wrote on Telegram. Thousands more people are listed as mi

stories.title: Germany's richest man takes on Big Tech
stories.summary: Supermarket billionaire Dieter Schwarz is investing billions in his cloud company, Schwarz Digits, to rival Google, Microsoft and Amazon.

stories.title: Heat is especially hard on people taking antidepressants
stories.summary: High temperatures can increase the risk of dizziness, dehydration, exhaustion and other heat-related problems for people taking antidepressants.

stories.title: Gas queues grow as Ukraine targets Russia's fuel supply
stories.summary: Attacks by Ukraine have triggered a fuel crisis in Russia. Reports from across Russia show how residents are dealing with the disruptions in their daily lives.

stories.title: Trump signals support for Patriot production in Ukraine
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump says he may let Ukraine produce Patriot air defense missiles. Analysts welcome the move as a sign of support for the country — but warn it could take years before production begins.

stories.title: Trump threatens to 'completely decimate' Iran, a day after claiming talks to take place
stories.summary: "1,000 missiles are locked and loaded and aimed at the Islamic Republic of Iran," Trump wrote on social media.

stories.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged theft of trade secrets - here's what to know
stories.summary: A spokesperson for OpenAI said in a statement: "We have no interest in other companies' trade secrets."

stories.title: Iran attacks Gulf states to impose its Hormuz battle lines
stories.summary: Iran launched waves of attacks across the Gulf in response to US airstrikes the night before, signalling the Islamic Republic’s refreshed strike capabilities, while claiming sole control of the Strait of Hormuz. The Gulf states are once again on the frontlines of the growing US-Iran confrontation.

stories.title: Former Qatar ruler Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani dies aged 74
stories.summary: Sheikh Hamad was known for having helped bring about huge economic, social, and cultural change in Qatar during his reign and helping establish the nation as a global player in diplomacy and investment.

stories.title: A rock star welcome and human rights protests to greet India’s prime minister Narendra Modi in Australia
stories.summary: Amnesty International says Albanese has opportunity to ‘reaffirm mutual commitment’ to rule of law as thousands in diaspora expected at stadium event Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Indian prime minister Narendra Modi’s first visit to Australia in three years has sparked calls for the federal government to raise human rights concerns, as excitement builds within the nation’s Indian diaspora. Modi’s visit begi

stories.title: Merz: Germany agreed to buy Tomahawk missiles at NATO summit
stories.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has informed the Bundestag parliament of a deal to buy US Tomahawk cruise missiles and station them in Germany. He said this closed a "important strategic ‌gap ​in our defense."

stories.title: EU rejects suspending biometric border controls despite 20 ‘difficult spots’
stories.summary: Officials admit new post-Brexit EES system is ‘not perfect’, as airports and airlines voice fears over delays The EU has rejected calls by airports and airlines to suspend the implementation of new fingerprinting and facial recognition border controls even though it admits there are “20 difficult spots” with queue chaos. With only a week to go before the peak summer holiday season starts, EU officials said the new entry/exit system (EES) was “not perfect” but it would tell travel industry repres

stories.title: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen says she will run for president in 2027 – video
stories.summary: Speaking in a prime-time interview on TF1 TV on Tuesday, the French far-right leader Marine Le Pen announced she will run for president in 2027 after an appeal court shortened her ban on holding public office. On Tuesday, the Paris appeals court upheld Le Pen's conviction for misusing European parliament funds but reduced the ban on running for office, clearing the way for the 57-year-old to stand in next year's election Continue reading...

stories.title: Furore in Nigeria over fake federal agency set up in government HQ
stories.summary: President orders investigation after fictitious body given funding, triggering renewed scrutiny of alleged corruption A fictitious federal entity that was allocated 1.3bn naira (£700,000) in Nigeria’s 2026 budget has precipitated a political storm in Africa’s largest democracy in the run-up to a general election set for January. The fake agency came to light last October when Femi Gbajabiamila, the president’s chief of staff, wrote to the police alleging that his signature, along with official s
